channel.title: Edgar's Personal Wiki
channel.description: Last 10 notes on Edgar's Personal Wiki

item.title: Edgar's Personal Wiki
item.description: Edgar’s Personal Wiki Public notebook This is the public edge of a private working wiki: source-backed notes about software, systems, books, institutions, and the moments where fiction explains reality better than reality explains itself.

item.title: ai-commissioning
item.description: AI Commissioning AI commissioning is the shift from using an AI system as a closely steered assistant to briefing an agentic model on an ambitious outcome, letting it execute through a long and partly hidden workflow, and then judging the delivered artifact.

item.title: recursive-self-improvement
item.description: Recursive Self-Improvement Recursive self-improvement is the threshold where an AI system can autonomously design and develop its own successor.

item.title: ethan-mollick
item.description: Ethan Mollick Ethan Mollick is the credited author of the One Useful Thing article What it feels like to work with Mythos.

item.title: what-it-feels-like-to-work-with-mythos
item.description: What It Feels Like To Work With Mythos What it feels like to work with Mythos is a 2026-06-09 One Useful Thing article by Ethan Mollick.

item.title: one-useful-thing
item.description: One Useful Thing One Useful Thing is currently represented in this wiki by Ethan Mollick’s article on working with a Mythos-class AI model.

item.title: gnss-interference
item.description: GNSS Interference GNSS interference disrupts satellite navigation systems such as GPS, GLONASS, Galileo, and BeiDou.

item.title: veritasium-gps-jamming-over-europe
item.description: Veritasium: Something is jamming GPS over Europe This June 5, 2026 Veritasium video explains a GNSS interference investigation that traces short, narrowband bursts over Europe to the Russian Cosmos 2546 satellite while preserving uncertainty about whether the signal was a jamming test, covert commun...

item.title: veritasium
item.description: Veritasium Veritasium is currently represented in this wiki by three technical explainer videos: one on the Aharonov-Bohm effect, one on shortest-path algorithms for map routing, and one on GNSS interference and satellite-navigation resilience.

item.title: choosing-what-to-work-on
item.description: Choosing What to Work On Choosing what to work on is the judgment of selecting problems whose importance, evidence, timing, and fit justify sustained attention.
